topic: A person finds out their partner has been learning their native language
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sound of the unfamiliar cadence hit me like a physical blow. I was trying to focus on a spreadsheet, the numbers swimming before my eyes, when he started speaking. Not in English, not in any of the languages we shared, but a fluid, melodic stream of my mother tongue. I lowered the laptop slowly, the click of the mouse sounding deafening in the sudden silence. I stared at him, my jaw tightening. "What are you doing?" I managed, my voice tight, barely above a whisper. He looked up, a bright, slightly bewildered smile on his face, and began to reply in rapid, complex sentences that made no sense to me. I pushed my chair back, the scrape echoing sharply across the floor. I needed air.

[story 2]

I watched him from across the kitchen island, the way his mouth moved, the subtle shift in his tone that betrayed the foreign sounds he was weaving together. Every syllable was a deliberate effort, a careful construction of something utterly alien to our shared world. My hands clenched into fists at my sides, my knuckles turning white. I took a slow, deliberate sip of my water, trying to keep my expression neutral, but a tightness coiled in my chest, making my breath shallow. When he finished a long, flowing sentence, I finally spoke, my tone clipped and flat. "Honey, can we just… stick to English for a bit?" The air between us thickened, heavy with unspoken frustration.

[story 3]

It was the casualness of it that grated on me the most. One moment, he was recounting a mundane story, and the next, he’d slip into a phrase, a perfectly formed idiom in my native tongue, completely unprompted. I stopped mid-sentence, my eyes narrowing as I processed the linguistic shift. A low growl escaped my throat, a sound I instantly regretted. I stood up abruptly, pacing three steps toward the window before spinning back around. "Stop," I commanded, my voice sharp enough to cut glass. I watched him, waiting, my entire posture radiating a profound sense of being overwhelmed by the unexpected intrusion.
